page.content: In the analysis of Fine Chemical batch yields for Air Liquide from 2020 to 2022, the data reveals significant trends across the examined years. The yield ratios for 2020 show Q1Yield at 85.5%, Q2Yield at 88.0%, Q3Yield at 87.2%, and Q4Yield remains unreported. In 2021, the ratios improve with Q1Yield at 90.1%, Q2Yield at 91.5%, Q3Yield at 89.8%, and Q4Yield again unreported. By 2022, the yield ratios further increase, with Q1Yield at 92.0%, Q2Yield at 93.5%, Q3Yield at 91.7%, and the Q4Yield once more unreported. The AverageYield for 2020 stands at 88.5%, for 2021 at 90.3%, and for 2022 at 91.5%. The ProductionCost demonstrates a decreasing trend, with costs at ₹1500 in 2020, ₹1400 in 2021, and ₹1350 in 2022. TotalOutput increases over the years, starting at 2000L in 2020, rising to 2200L in 2021, and reaching 2500L in 2022. MarketShare also shows growth, with figures of 15.0% for 2020, 16.5% for 2021, and 17.0% for 2022. CustomerSatisfaction improves from 85% in 2020 to 87% in 2021, culminating at 90% in 2022. NetProfit exhibits a positive trajectory, with ₹300M in 2020, ₹320M in 2021, and ₹350M in 2022, reflecting overall improvement in operational performance across the analyzed period.
